brief_justification: Correct answer is Yes but agent concluded No (~36%), misinterpreting the question as asking for probability of necessary causation rather than whether jyka was present and caused yupt given the high P(J=1)=88% and P(Y=1|J=1)=92%; Reasoning is multi-layered with do-calculus, counterfactual analysis, and monotonicity assumptions showing genuine depth; Some self-monitoring via the RA²R injection and noting backdoor paths but didn't question whether the 50% threshold framing was appropriate; Verification through explicit computation and sanity-checking the collider structure; Acknowledges the baseline rate uncertainty and its impact but presents the wrong conclusion confidently; Considers the counterfactual decomposition as an alternative to simple correlation but doesn't consider that the question may simply ask about P(J=1, Y=1 | do(K=0)); Full derivation chain is reproducible with all steps shown clearly.
